task.prompt: The Context.Copy() method in Gin has a data race. It reads c.Keys into a local variable before acquiring the read lock, but another goroutine calling c.Set() may reassign c.Keys from nil to a new map at the same time. The unsynchronized read of c.Keys races with the write in Set(), causing maps.Clone() to operate on a stale or partially-initialized map pointer. This can produce silent data corruption or a panic under concurrent access. Investigate the mutex ordering in Copy() relative to Set() and fix the synchronization.

hint_levels.symptom_only: Under high concurrency, calling Copy() on a gin.Context while another goroutine calls Set() causes intermittent panics or silently returns a Context with missing keys. The race detector flags a concurrent read/write on the keys map field.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug is in context.go in the Copy() method at lines 125-128. The line cKeys := c.Keys reads the field before c.mu.RLock() is called on the next line. Compare with Set() at lines 453-461 which holds the write lock while potentially reassigning c.Keys from nil to a new map.
hint_levels.function_hint: In context.go Copy() at line 125, cKeys := c.Keys reads the field outside the lock. At line 126, c.mu.RLock() acquires the lock too late. In Set() at line 457, c.Keys = make(map[any]any) writes the field under the write lock. The fix is to move the read of c.Keys inside the RLock/RUnlock block so maps.Clone operates on a consistent snapshot.

reasoning_chain: Read symptom: data race between Copy() and Set() on Context.Keys
Open context.go and find Copy() method at lines 111-130
Notice line 125: cKeys := c.Keys reads the field BEFORE c.mu.RLock() on line 126
Find Set() method at lines 453-461 — acquires write lock and may assign c.Keys = make(map[any]any) if nil
Open gin.go and find ServeHTTP — reset() sets c.Keys = nil, then handlers run and call Set()
Realize: concurrent Copy() reads c.Keys outside lock while Set() writes it under lock — textbook race
Open routergroup.go to confirm middleware can call Set/Get concurrently
Apply fix: move cKeys := c.Keys inside the c.mu.RLock()/c.mu.RUnlock() block
task.gold_patch:
--- a/context.go
+++ b/context.go
@@ -122,8 +122,9 @@ func (c *Context) Copy() *Context {
 	cp := *c
 	cp.writermem.ResponseWriter = nil
 	cp.Writer = &cp.writermem
-	cKeys := c.Keys
 	c.mu.RLock()
+	cKeys := c.Keys
 	cp.Keys = maps.Clone(cKeys)
 	c.mu.RUnlock()
 	cp.index = abortIndex
